title: numpy>=2.0 breaks CANN 8.1.RC1 TBE compiler — pin to numpy<2.0
problem_description: numpy 2.0+ introduced breaking API changes (removed np.bool, np.int, np.float aliases and changed several array protocol behaviors) that are incompatible with the Ascend Tensor Boost Engine (TBE) compiler in CANN 8.1.RC1. This causes cryptic compilation failures or silent errors during torch_npu kernel execution. pip default resolution picks the latest numpy (2.x) unless explicitly constrained.
rough_fix_approach: After installing torch_npu and before running any model code, verify numpy version with `python -c 'import numpy; print(numpy.__version__)'`. If version >= 2.0.0, downgrade: `pip install 'numpy>=1.26,<2.0'`. numpy 1.26.4 is the last known-good release for CANN 8.1.RC1.
artifact_evidence: reports/SUMMARY_REPORT.md: line 104 'numpy>=2.0 incompatible with CANN 8.1.RC1 TBE — Pinned to numpy==1.26.4'
reports/TOOLS_EXECUTION_REPORT.md: line 37 shows explicit 'pip install numpy==1.26.4' as command 11 in the fix sequence
execution_journal.jsonl: phase_2_venv_create duration 369s reflects trial-and-error dependency resolution

involved_code_files.role: Entry script — imports numpy at line 23; numpy is also a transitive dependency of torch_npu which internally calls TBE for kernel compilation

reasoning: CANN version pinning to numpy <2.0 is a known compatibility constraint for the entire Ascend TBE stack. It generalizes beyond this project: any CANN 8.1.RC1 deployment will fail with numpy>=2.0. Priority is high because numpy version resolution happens early in Phase 2 and failure here blocks all subsequent validation.
